args.name: project
args.description: Specifies the project file. If not specified, the command searches the current directory for one

name: -f
--framework
options.description: Adds a package reference only when targeting a specific framework

options.name: --interactive
options.description: Allows the command to stop and wait for user input or action. For example, to complete authentication

name: -n
--no-restore
options.description: Adds a package reference without performing a restore preview and compatibility check

options.name: --package-directory
options.description: The directory where to restore the packages. The default package restore location is %userprofile%.nugetpackages on Windows and ~/.nuget/packages on macOS and Linux

options.name: --prerelease
options.description: Allows prerelease packages to be installed. Available since .NET Core 5 SDK

name: -s
--source
options.description: The URI of the NuGet package source to use during the restore operation

subcommands.name: reference
subcommands.description: The dotnet add reference command provides a convenient option to add project references to a project. After running the command, the <ProjectReference> elements are added to the project file

name: -f
--framework
options.description: Adds a package reference only when targeting a specific framework
